headline: Penang Undersea Tunnel Project Breaks Ground After 13 Years
context: Penang undersea tunnel project broke ground in March 2026 thirteen years after initial feasibility study with final cost of RM6.3 billion.

cards.big: Penang undersea tunnel breaks ground after 13 years at RM6.3 billion cost
cards.sub: 7.2km tunnel connecting George Town to Butterworth targets 2031 completion

cards.big: Original 2013 feasibility estimated RM4.5 billion for the tunnel component
cards.sub: Cost escalation of 40% attributed to material prices and design complexity

cards.big: Tunnel designed for 50,000 vehicles daily reducing Penang Bridge congestion 25%
cards.sub: Environmental groups raised concerns about seabed disruption in channel ecology

cards.big: Contractor consortium includes 3 Malaysian and 2 South Korean firms
cards.sub: State government funding 40% with federal allocation covering remaining 60%

cards.big: Tunnel groundbreaking tests whether mega-projects can finally move from study to steel
13 years to break ground on Penang's undersea tunnel. Mega-projects test whether governance can match ambition.
